title: Raw-Categorical CatBoost Specialist With Ordered Statistics
summary: Current best solutions are feature-heavy and likely tree-ensemble dominated, but CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are specifically designed to reduce prediction shift and leakage with high-cardinality categoricals like Driver, Race, and their interactions.
rationale: Current best solutions are feature-heavy and likely tree-ensemble dominated, but CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are specifically designed to reduce prediction shift and leakage with high-cardinality categoricals like Driver, Race, and their interactions.
implementation_hint: Run a separate CatBoost classifier on mostly raw columns plus a small number of robust numeric interactions, preserving categorical columns directly and letting ordered CTRs learn `Driver x Race`, `Race x Compound`, and `Driver x Compound` structure. Keep validation grouped by race/year and avoid external one-hot or naive target encoding.
expected_effect: A complementary specialist may capture categorical interaction signal the current handcrafted-window models miss, improving ensemble diversity more than standalone CV.
risk: If chronology is not respected in folds or rare categories dominate, CatBoost can still overfit; compare carefully against the current best single model.
